sentences.text: Hey Brian.
Hey, how's it going?
Not too bad.
How are you doing?
I'm doing great, just got back from Mexico, so.
Back in the
cold.

Yeah I was on a phone call with one of my investors this morning.
He was in Michigan, he said it was like 9 degrees.
Yeah, it's uh we've gotten, it's all, all the winds have shifted and they're coming out of the, the northwest from Canada, so it's never a good when that happens.
It's been like uh probably down into the single digits for the last like week and a half here.
So,
um, are you, are you from there or did you move there?
I'm from here and just made the bad decision to move back.
I moved to, I actually lived out in Denver for probably almost 5 years and then just kind of moved back here.
So, uh, yeah, it's uh my choice, but every like now as I get a little bit older, it's this time of the year I'm like, why do I still stay here?

And he has got invited to be a a a professor in the real estate school there.
Oh nice.
The, the real estate school is really second to none.
It's, uh, I know it's highly regarded in the industry,
um.

Wisconsin right now is #1.
OK, awesome.
Well, that's really what position in baseball.
He's a pitcher.
OK, really cool.

Nice, but yeah, I, I mean that's can't beat that.
That's a nice school to go to, so I'm sure he's pumped about that.

He kind of eased into the fall.
The true test is gonna be the spring when he's really playing.

Right, yeah, my, uh, my roommates in in the house that I lived in, uh, some played football, some played baseball, and the baseball guys in the spring, man, they, they missed so much class with uh like the double headers and, you know, just they fit that schedule in in like two months, it seems like.
Yeah, he's not allowed to take any Friday classes.
Because they travel, NYU plays all over the place, and they fly everywhere that they play, so he has to work his schedule around baseball, which is fine.
Yeah, well that that sounds exciting.
I mean you're gonna be able to travel quite a bit and catch the games, or yeah,

for sure.

that's really cool.

we're in Philly.

we're in a little suburb right outside Philadelphia.

really cool, um.

I was the president and CEO of the Gamma Group for 28 years.
OK.
Um,
uh, I don't know if you do you know them at all?

We had
8000 units under management and ownership.

So I've been here a little bit over a year,
um, but heavily in the real estate space.

And uh Altman, you know, is a good, good fit.
So why don't you give me the, I'm gonna put you on mute cause I have a couple of things going on.
I jump on another call in like 10 minutes.
I'll let you give me the rap and then you'll let me know how we go to the next level.

Yeah, um, I guess the big, you know, the big thing about apartment IQ and, you know, speaking with Lisa and a few other folks, um, at, at your, at your portfolio, it sounded like, you know, gathering data as well as then you set the rent rates, is that kind of correct?
It sounded like it was kind of a manual process.
Yeah, the, the A stuff is done through Yardi's rent max,
right,
and then the other stuff, uh CFO and I set the rents.
Which they were kind of having the regionals do it for a long time, so it's really nice for me because they're really under market so I get to raise the rents a ton.
Yeah, that's gotta be a good position then, uh, I guess just as far as setting those then, what are you looking, are you mainly just relying on Yardi or the market surveys?
What do you usually look at or go on the websites?
I know is what a lot of our partners have been doing, I guess what,
what are you doing?
Yeah, we do, we do twofold.
We do, we do the market comp study and also that he already has a platform.
That helps us, but we're definitely, um, you know, we're definitely missing opportunity.
Like, you know, doing that kind of how manual we're doing it, and we're aware of that.
Yeah, sure.

So I guess it, it, you know, don't have to, you know, beat that dead horse and so as far as what apartment IQ does is to the point is we automate that whole process for you and our information is updated on the daily.
So um you can get as high level as you want, but we also get as granular as unit level information so you're able to.
See all the way down the specific units, you know, rent rates, price per square foot, concession details, and then all your performance metrics.
So you're able to see, you know, your occupancy, least percentage, exposure, applicants within a week, um, all that really important performance metrics, um, you're still able to gather as well.
Um, and then we have, so we have, it's kind of, uh, it's.

Definitely a multi-use tool, so it's something that could be used from your onsite level, but then all the way up to executives like yourself, uh, we have more in-depth reports as well, so it's uh can show price changes by specific thresholds.
So if you wanted to literally filter down to you.

a specific like 1000 ft to 1200 ft, two bed, two bath, um, you could do that and see all the actual available versus lease rent rates, your prices square foot, average days of market, um, all those factors that go into pricing.
And it allows you to make those decisions that are, you know, just much more informed and as of today.
So, um, you know, if your onsite teams are doing that, you know, it's probably at least 1 week to 2 weeks old, and um this is gonna keep up to date with all those daily price changes.
We have complete nationwide coverage, so it's um gonna be able to cover any market.
It's literally anybody with a website, so what we're doing is we're having a proprietary scrapers go out and collect information.
So whether they are real page, andrata, resin, at Folio, etc.
um, doesn't matter if we're gonna be able to capture their information for you, um.
And then you have complete autonomy to add and remove your comp sets, which is a big advantage as well.
Um, so you can, you know, you're not locked into, you know, just like a certain, you know, 7 to 8 comps.
Um, you can see an entire market from a high level as well, so we have If you know, it sounds like you.

So actively investing and looking at deals, we also have an acquisitions and development tool within the app, so you're able to literally search any property uh nationwide, get a real-time market survey for that property, you know, benchmark versus the market, you know, what's it's, what's it been like over um all the way up to 4 years.
So we've been over a 10 year old company here and then.
Capturing data and storing it for 4 years, so you have up to 4 years of historical data as well.

Yeah,
we, we have around 10,000 units.
OK, so, um, with that, I mean, you'd be
